formulas.name: Imagery
formulas.formula: Visual representation of emotions, ideas, and sensations
formulas.variables: Use of sensory details to create vivid pictures in the reader's mind
formulas.notes: Helps to create a specific atmosphere and enhance the reader's experience

formulas.name: Symbolism
formulas.formula: Representation of abstract ideas through objects, colors, or other elements
formulas.variables: Use of symbols to convey deeper meaning and connections
formulas.notes: Helps to add depth and complexity to the narrative

formulas.name: Allusion
formulas.formula: Reference to a person, place, event, or work of art outside the text
formulas.variables: Use of allusions to create connections and build meaning
formulas.notes: Helps to enrich the narrative and engage the reader

formulas.name: Irony
formulas.formula: Discrepancy or incongruity between what is expected and what actually occurs
formulas.variables: Use of irony to highlight contradictions or create humor
formulas.notes: Helps to add complexity and depth to the narrative

formulas.name: Juxtaposition
formulas.formula: Placing two or more elements side by side to compare or contrast them
formulas.variables: Use of juxtaposition to highlight similarities and differences
formulas.notes: Helps to create a sense of tension or emphasize a point

formulas.name: Foreshadowing
formulas.formula: Hinting at events that will occur later in the narrative
formulas.variables: Use of foreshadowing to create suspense or tension
formulas.notes: Helps to engage the reader and build anticipation

formulas.name: Theme
formulas.formula: Central idea or message of the narrative
formulas.variables: Use of themes to convey meaning and connect with the reader
formulas.notes: Helps to add depth and complexity to the narrative

formulas.name: Characterization
formulas.formula: Description and development of characters in the narrative
formulas.variables: Use of characterization to create believable and relatable characters
formulas.notes: Helps to engage the reader and build connections with the characters

formulas.name: Tone
formulas.formula: Author's attitude or mood towards the subject matter
formulas.variables: Use of tone to create a specific atmosphere and engage the reader
formulas.notes: Helps to add depth and complexity to the narrative
